commands.review_summary:
The PR strips local symbols from release-packaged app, helper, CLI, and widget executables, adds a portable packaging-strip check to lint, and adds one changelog entry.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a release-packaging cleanup PR, not a bug report. The PR body gives after-change package, signing, launch, UUID, and size evidence instead of a failing reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review and release-machine validation.

target.title: Reduce release app bundle size
target.body:
## Summary

- strip local symbols from packaged release app, CLI, watchdog, and widget executables before signing
- preserve unstripped debug packages for local debugging
- add a portable packaging-script regression check to `make check`
- document the release-size improvement and credit @jieshu666

## Why

Swift symbol tables dominate the packaged executables. Release-only `strip -x` substantially reduces the installed app and download size while preserving the Mach-O UUID used to match the existing dSYM.

## Exact-head verification

Verified at `58e3da185ea3607c6a4cbd39d546c90c0df74894`:

- `./Scripts/test_package_strip.sh`
- `make check`
- autoreview: clean (0.86), no accepted/actionable findings
- `ARCHES=arm64 CODEXBAR_SIGNING=adhoc ./Scripts/package_app.sh release`
- deep strict code-sign verification passed
- app, CLI, watchdog, and widget executables contained no local symbols after packaging
- packaged app executable: 35,422,168 bytes before stripping; 18,161,360 bytes after stripping
- packaged app and dSYM UUID: `D9C0056F-A904-3F22-AE28-CFCD4DDC586B`
- packaged CLI version/help passed
- freshly packaged app launched and remained running

The contributor's universal release comparison measured the app zip shrinking from 48,598,232 to 38,769,894 bytes, approximately 20.2%.
target.author: jieshu666
